content.content:
=== PLAN ===
# pl24r — Pascal P-Code Linker in Rust

## Goal

Build a Rust CLI tool that combines multiple `.spc` (symbolic p-code assembler) text files into one merged `.spc` file for pasm assembly. This is a Phase 1 offline text-level linker for the Pascal-on-COR24 toolchain.

## Pipeline

```
Pascal source(s) → p24p compiler → .spc file(s)  ─┐
Hand-written runtime .spc (phase0)                 ├→ pl24r linker → combined .spc → pasm → .p24 → emulator
Pascal-compiled runtime .spc (phase1+)             ┘
```

## Input categories

1. **App** — the main program module (compiled from Pascal by p24p)
2. **User library** (optional) — separately compiled Pascal library
3. **Phase 0 runtime** — hand-written .spc (write_int, write_ln, write_bool)
4. **Phase 1+ runtime** — Pascal-compiled runtime routines

## Module metadata format (co-designed with pv24a/p24p)

New `.spc` directives for linking:

- `.module Name` — declare module identity
- `.export sym` — mark symbol as visible to other modules
- `.extern sym` — declare external symbol dependency
- `.endmodule` — end module boundary

**Fallback**: files without metadata are treated as export-all (all `.proc`, `.global`, `.data` symbols exported).

## Output

One combined `.spc` text file with all modules merged, all symbols resolved, ready for pasm. No `.pco` or `.pex` intermediate formats.

## Design constraints

- Text in, text out — no binary formats
- Transport-neutral (files, stdin, pipes)
- Line-oriented, forward-readable
- Entry point: the `main` proc from the app module
- Duplicate symbol detection with clear errors
- Unresolved extern detection with clear errors

## Steps

1. **001-spc-parser** — Rust project scaffold + .spc text parser
2. **002-symbol-table** — Symbol collection, export/import resolution, validation
3. **003-linker-core** — Module merging, ordering, combined .spc emission
4. **004-cli-integration** — CLI interface, file/stream modes, error reporting
5. **005-pipeline-test** — End-to-end test with real runtime + app through full pipeline

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-spc-parser [production]: Create the pl24r Rust linker project scaffold with edition 2024. Set up src/m...
  [x] 002-symbol-table [production]: Build the symbol table and validation layer in src/symbols.rs. Collect all sy...
  [ ] 003-linker-core [production]: Implement the core linker in src/linker.rs that merges multiple parsed .spc m... <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 004-cli-integration [production]: Build the CLI interface in src/main.rs. Support two modes: (1) file mode: pl2...
  [ ] 005-pipeline-test [production]: End-to-end integration test with the real Pascal toolchain. (1) Copy or refer...

=== CURRENT STEP: 003-linker-core [production] ===
Status: pending
Description: Implement the core linker in src/linker.rs that merges multiple parsed .spc m...
Task type: rust-project-init

=== PROMPT ===
Implement the core linker in src/linker.rs that merges multiple parsed .spc modules into one combined .spc text output. The linker must: (1) order modules correctly — runtime modules first, then user libraries, then app module last so main is the entry point, (2) strip .module/.endmodule/.export/.extern directives from output since they are linker-only metadata, (3) merge all .global declarations into one section at the top, (4) merge all .data declarations, (5) merge all .const declarations (checking for conflicts), (6) concatenate all .proc/.end blocks preserving internal labels, (7) preserve comments for debuggability, (8) ensure the output is valid standalone .spc that pasm can assemble directly. The emitter in src/emitter.rs should produce clean, well-formatted .spc text output. Test: cargo test with multi-module merge scenarios, verify output parses back cleanly, verify correct ordering. cargo clippy -- -D warnings must pass.

=== PAST SUCCESSES (rust-project-init) ===
  [2026-03-26T01:28:33] action=Wrote symbols.rs with Symbol/SymbolTable types and build_symbol_table() function, fixed clippy overly_complex_bool_expr warning, result=success, reward=+1
  [2026-03-26T01:26:06] action=Read reference .spc files, wrote parser.rs with full AST types and recursive-descent parser, wrote unit tests, fixed clippy warnings, result=success, reward=+1

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

input.command:
git -C /Users/mike/github/softwarewrighter/pl24r add src/linker.rs src/lib.rs && git -C /Users/mike/github/softwarewrighter/pl24r commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(linker): implement core linker with module merging and .spc emission

Add src/linker.rs with link() and emit() functions that merge multiple
parsed .spc modules into a single pasm-ready output. Modules are ordered
with runtime/libraries first and the app module (containing main) last.
Globals are deduplicated, constants are conflict-checked, linker metadata
(.module/.export/.extern/.endmodule) is stripped, and comments are preserved.
Includes 13 tests covering ordering, merging, conflict detection, roundtrip
parsing, and three-module integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
